content:
🔥 geo-optimizer-skill | Python
🎯 Primary Use Case: Optimizing websites to be discoverable and citable by AI search engines.
✨ Key Features: • Audit websites against 47 AI search engine visibility methods • Generate actionable recommendations for optimization • Create missing SEO files (robots.txt, llms.txt, schema, meta) • Compare website versions and detect regressions • Monitor AI visibility and track trends over time
📖 Summary: The GEO Optimizer is a Python toolkit designed to audit, optimize, and enhance website visibility for AI search engines like ChatGPT, Perplexity, Claude, and Gemini. It analyzes websites based on 47 research-backed methods to ensure they are discoverable and citable by AI.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

🚀 WireTapper | HTML
🎯 Primary Use Case: Passive wireless reconnaissance and signal intelligence gathering for OSINT and security analysis.
✨ Key Features: • Passively detects Wi-Fi, Bluetooth, CCTV cameras, vehicles, headphones, TVs, IoT devices, and cell towers • Maps and analyzes radio signals for situational intelligence • Identifies leaked Wi-Fi network credentials using k-Anonymity
📖 Summary: WireTapper is a wireless OSINT and signal intelligence platform that passively detects, maps, and analyzes radio signals from various devices like Wi-Fi, Bluetooth, cameras, vehicles, and IoT appliances. It aims to provide situational intelligence by understanding the invisible wireless landscape without active intrusion.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source

🔥 lynxdb | Go
🎯 Primary Use Case: Analyzing and querying log data in real-time or from files, with a focus on ease of use and performance through a single binary deployment.
✨ Key Features: • Single binary, no dependencies • Pipe mode for ad-hoc analysis • Server mode for persistent operation • Lynx Flow query language (SPL2 compatible) • Full-text search with FST inverted index
📖 Summary: LynxDB is a lightweight, single-binary log analytics tool that processes and queries log data using its proprietary Lynx Flow query language. It supports both a pipe mode for ad-hoc analysis directly from standard input and a server mode for persistent data ingestion and querying, offering features like full-text search and columnar storage.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

🌟 SpotiFLAC-RU | TypeScript
🎯 Primary Use Case: To provide Russian-speaking users with a localized tool to download high-quality FLAC audio tracks from Spotify, sourced from other premium music streaming services.
✨ Key Features: • Downloads Spotify tracks in true FLAC quality • Sources audio from Tidal, Qobuz, and Amazon Music • No Spotify account required • Full Russian interface translation
📖 Summary: SpotiFLAC-RU is a Russian localization of a tool that downloads Spotify tracks in FLAC quality from various streaming services like Tidal, Qobuz, and Amazon Music without requiring a user account. This fork specifically offers a fully translated interface and localized notifications for Russian-speaking users, along with fixes for Windows compilation issues.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source

🌟 kiali | Go
🎯 Primary Use Case: Providing observability and a management console for Istio service meshes.
✨ Key Features: • Management console for Istio service mesh • Observability and insights into mesh behavior • Installation as an Istio add-on or production integration • Developer-focused documentation and setup guides • AI Documentation (Dev Preview)
📖 Summary: Kiali is a management console for Istio service meshes, providing observability and insights into the mesh's behavior. It can be installed as an Istio add-on or integrated into production environments, offering features for developers to understand and manage their service mesh.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source
